items.front: When must an ASDE system be operated?
items.back: Determine its status at relief. Systems with safety logic run continuously; systems without safety logic run continuously at night, in reduced visibility, or whenever they help augment visual observation.

items.front: How is a target on an ASDE display identified or treated as false?
items.back: Identify it by correlating it with a position report, controller visual observation, or an identified ASR or CTRD target. A verified false target may be temporarily dropped and logged on FAA Form 7230-4.

items.front: How may ASDE-derived information be used, and what is prohibited?
items.back: Use ASDE information for movement instructions, position, compliance, and taxi directions. Do not give headings except in an emergency or by agreement, or allow departure or landing over an unidentified runway target.

items.front: What is the immediate response to an ASDE safety-logic alert?
items.back: Assess it visually and on the display at once. Warning alerts involving arrivals before the threshold or to a taxiway require a go-around; other alerts require appropriate action and judgment.
